# Product Vision

Your team is building a **financial technology product**: a domain-scoped Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that enables analysts to ask structured questions about publicly traded companies in a specific industry and receive grounded, citation-backed answers drawn from SEC 10-K annual filings.

This is not a research exercise. Think of the final deliverable as a prototype you would present to a FinTech startup, an investment analytics team, or a regulatory technology firm. The product must be reproducible, auditable, and domain-coherent.

Primary workflow:

- Google Colab notebooks
- Google Drive storage

Optional workflow:

- Local machine execution
- GitHub version control (optional, not required for AD698)

:::{.callout-important}
GitHub is optional in AD698. Do not block progress if your team is not using GitHub.
:::

# NAICS Industry Code Selection

Your team must select a **specific 4-digit NAICS (North American Industry Classification System) code** to scope your product. All company selection, filing downloads, and analytical questions must be anchored to this single industry.

Use the official NAICS reference document to browse industry categories and choose one that is analytically interesting and has at least 8-10 publicly traded companies filing 10-Ks:

> [NAICS Industry Code Reference (PDF)](https://www.uscourts.gov/file/78724/download)

Your NAICS code selection must be documented at the top of your first notebook and justified in 2-3 sentences: why is this industry relevant to financial analysis or FinTech applications?

**Examples of analytically rich NAICS 4-digit codes:**

| NAICS Code | Industry |
|---:|---|
| 5221 | Depository Credit Intermediation (Banking) |
| 5241 | Insurance Carriers |
| 5179 | Other Telecommunications |
| 3674 | Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing |
| 5112 | Software Publishers |
| 5242 | Agencies, Brokerages, and Other Insurance Related |
| 3559 | Special Industry Machinery Manufacturing |
| 4911 | Electric Power Generation, Transmission and Distribution |

# Complete Product Pipeline

Your FinTech product is built in four sequential steps across Modules 1-5. Each step produces artifacts that feed the next.

## Step 1 — Industry Scoping, Metadata, and Filing Acquisition (M1)

- Select NAICS 4-digit industry and 5-10 public companies
- Download 10-K HTML filings from SEC EDGAR for 2015-2025
- Parse SEC header metadata and DEI/XBRL fields
- Extract all standard 10-K Items into file-level artifacts
- **Deliverable:** `filing_metadata.csv`, extracted item files

## Step 2 — Item Cleaning and Exploratory Data Analysis (M2)

- Select domain-relevant 10-K Items tied to your NAICS industry
- Clean raw HTML text: remove boilerplate, table-of-contents noise, artifacts
- Conduct EDA: token distributions, company and year coverage, top terms
- **Deliverable:** `cleaned_item_metadata.csv`, cleaned item text files

## Step 3 — Chunking, Embeddings, and Vector Index (M3)

- Chunk cleaned text with token-aware overlap strategy
- Generate embeddings via API or open-source model
- Build a FAISS vector index with full chunk-level metadata
- Validate top-k retrieval quality on test questions
- **Deliverable:** `sec10k_chunks.parquet`, vector index artifacts

## Step 4 — Grounded Generation, Market Analysis, and Evaluation (M5)

- Build a grounded RAG prompt pipeline with citation requirements
- Generate answers to industry-relevant financial questions
- Fetch historical stock prices for your selected companies using the Yahoo Finance API and conduct a meaningful market-performance analysis tied to your RAG findings
- Evaluate retrieval relevance, answer faithfulness, and hallucination risk
- Package final report with findings and limitations
- **Deliverable:** `rag_answers.csv`, `rag_evaluation.csv`, `yahoo_prices/`, final report

:::{.callout-note}
Module 4 is a mid-project checkpoint. Use that week to catch up, clean up, and confirm Steps 1-3 are complete before starting Step 4.
:::

# Project Folder Structure

Set up your project folder before writing any code. Use the structure below whether you are working in Google Drive (Colab) or a local Git repository.

```
project_sec10k_rag/
├── README.md
├── requirements.txt
├── .env                        ← local API keys (never commit)
├── .gitignore
├── notebooks/
│   ├── 01_industry_company_selection_and_10k_download.ipynb
│   ├── 02_item_selection_cleaning_and_eda.ipynb
│   ├── 03_embeddings_and_rag_preparation.ipynb
│   └── 04_rag_generation_and_evaluation.ipynb
├── scripts/
│   ├── parse_sec_metadata.py
│   ├── download_10k_html.py
│   ├── extract_10k_items.py
│   ├── clean_items.py
│   ├── build_embeddings.py
│   ├── build_vector_index.py
│   ├── rag_pipeline.py
│   ├── evaluate_rag.py
│   └── download_yahoo_prices.py
├── data/
│   ├── raw_html/
│   ├── extracted_items/
│   ├── cleaned_items/
│   ├── chunked/
│   ├── embeddings/
│   ├── vector_store/
│   ├── yahoo_prices/           ← stock price data from Yahoo Finance API
│   └── outputs/
├── config/
│   ├── companies.csv
│   ├── industry_selection.yaml
│   ├── item_map.yaml
│   ├── model_config.yaml
│   └── sic_naics_map.csv
└── reports/
    ├── figures/
    ├── tables/
    └── final_outputs/
```

**Google Drive users:** create this folder at `MyDrive/project_sec10k_rag/` and mirror the `data/`, `config/`, and `reports/` sub-structure. Notebooks live in the Drive root or a `notebooks/` subfolder.

**Git users:** initialize the repo at the `project_sec10k_rag/` root. Add `.env`, `data/raw_html/`, `data/embeddings/`, and `data/vector_store/` to `.gitignore`.

:::{.callout-tip}
Create the folder structure in your first notebook cell before writing any data so that all subsequent notebooks can reference `BASE_DIR` without path errors.
:::

# Step 1 Deliverable Scope

In Step 1, your team must:

- confirm NAICS 4-digit code selection and document industry rationale
- choose 5-10 publicly traded companies in that NAICS industry
- download 10-K HTML filings for 2015-2025 from SEC EDGAR
- parse SEC header and DEI/XBRL metadata
- extract all standard 10-K Items
- save item-level files with standardized names

# Colab Setup (Required)

## Mount Drive

::: {#68f807e3 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from google.colab import drive
drive.mount('/content/drive')
```
:::


## Base Path Pattern

::: {#909bd7f4 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from pathlib import Path

BASE_DIR = Path('/content/drive/MyDrive/project_sec10k_rag')
# Optional local alternative:
# BASE_DIR = Path.cwd() / 'project_sec10k_rag'

RAW_HTML_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'raw_html'
EXTRACTED_ITEMS_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'extracted_items'
OUTPUTS_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'outputs'

for d in [RAW_HTML_DIR, EXTRACTED_ITEMS_DIR, OUTPUTS_DIR]:
    d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
```
:::


# Required SEC Metadata Fields

Your filing metadata table must contain:
- accession_number
- form_type
- filing_date
- acceptance_datetime
- period_of_report
- company_name
- cik
- sic_code
- sic_description
- organization_name
- fiscal_year_end
- state_of_incorporation
- irs_number
- sec_file_number
- film_number
- business_city
- business_state
- business_zip
- document_type
- document_period_end_date
- entity_file_number
- filer_category
- public_float
- shares_outstanding
- source_file
- generated_filename

# Filename Standards

Filing HTML filename convention:
- YYYYMMDD_10-K_CIK_ACCESSION_COMPANY-NAME-(TICKER).html

Extracted item filename convention:
- YYYYMMDD_10-K_CIK_ACCESSION_TICKER_item_1.html

# Required Outputs for Step 1

- data/outputs/filing_metadata.csv
- data/outputs/industry_filing_panel.csv
- extracted item files in data/extracted_items/

# Security and Secrets Management

Your product will use paid API services (LLMs, embeddings, potentially Yahoo Finance premium). Never hard-code API keys in notebook cells or commit them to any repository.

## Colab Secrets (Colab workflow)

Use the Colab Secrets panel (the key icon in the left sidebar) to store keys, then load them at runtime:

::: {#787fae80 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from google.colab import userdata
OPENAI_API_KEY = userdata.get('OPENAI_API_KEY')
GEMINI_API_KEY = userdata.get('GEMINI_API_KEY')
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = userdata.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY')
COHERE_API_KEY = userdata.get('COHERE_API_KEY')
```
:::


## .env File (local or Git workflow)

For local development, store all secrets in a `.env` file at the project root and load with `python-dotenv`:

```
# .env  ← never commit this file
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GEMINI_API_KEY=...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...
COHERE_API_KEY=...
```

::: {#6d9afda5 .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os

load_dotenv()  # reads .env from the current working directory
OPENAI_API_KEY = os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')
```
:::


Add `.env` to `.gitignore` immediately:

```
# .gitignore
.env
data/raw_html/
data/embeddings/
data/vector_store/
```

:::{.callout-warning}
Never commit secrets, raw HTML filings, embeddings, or vector store artifacts to any public or private repository.
:::

# Student Submission Checklist (Step 1)

- [ ] NAICS 4-digit code selected and justified
- [ ] 5-10 companies selected with metadata
- [ ] 2015-2025 filings downloaded
- [ ] Required SEC metadata fields extracted
- [ ] Filename conventions followed
- [ ] `filing_metadata.csv` and `industry_filing_panel.csv` exported

# Weekly Team Standup

Your team holds a **5-minute standup once per week** during Module 1. This mirrors the daily standup ritual used in agile software and FinTech product teams. The goal is to surface blockers early, keep everyone aligned, and give the instructor visibility into team progress.

> **Reference:** [Atlassian — How to Run Effective Standups](https://www.atlassian.com/agile/scrum/standups)

Each standup covers three questions for the team — not individuals:

1. **What did the team complete since the last standup?**
2. **What will the team work on before the next standup?**
3. **What blockers or decisions is the team stuck on?**

## In-Person Sections

Standup happens live at the start of class. One team member facilitates, each person answers the three questions in ≤60 seconds, and the team flags one blocker or open decision for instructor input.

## Online Sections — Weekly Standup Document

Submit a **one-page standup document** to the course portal by the due date each week. This is ungraded on its own, but **standup submissions count toward your final project participation grade**.

Use this template:

---

**Team Name / NAICS Industry:**

**Standup Week:** Module 1 — Week ___

**Date Submitted:**

| Question | Team Response |
|---|---|
| What did we complete this week? | |
| What will we work on next week? | |
| What is blocking us or needs a decision? | |

**Files or outputs produced this week** (list filenames or Drive links):

**Team member who facilitated this standup:**

---

:::{.callout-note}
Standup documents should be honest and brief — one paragraph per row is enough. If there are no blockers, say so explicitly. Instructors read these to anticipate where teams need support before the next class.
:::
